metadata.title: States PCS: Gujarat GK Questions for GPSC Exams

questions.question: Which district has the smallest population in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Dang district is the smallest district in Gujarat by population, located in the southern part of the state.

questions.question: On which river is the Kakrapar Dam in Gujarat constructed?
questions.explanation: Kakrapar Dam, located near Kakrapar village in Surat district, Gujarat, is built on the Tapi River for irrigation.

questions.question: In which district of Gujarat is the Damanganga Project, featuring the Madhuban Dam, located?
questions.explanation: Gujarat’s significant irrigation project, the Damanganga Project, is located in Valsad district, featuring the Madhuban Dam and serving irrigation, water supply, and hydroelectric power needs in Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Union Territories.

questions.question: Which building in London was established by Indian nationalists to support the Indian independence movement?
questions.explanation: India House in London was established by Indian nationalists to support the Indian independence movement.

questions.question: On which river was the Shyam Sarovar reservoir in Gujarat constructed?
questions.explanation: Shyam Sarovar reservoir in Gujarat was created by constructing a dam on the Meshwo River, primarily for irrigation purposes.

questions.question: Who led the movement to close foreign cloth and liquor shops in Ahmedabad during the Civil Disobedience Movement?
questions.explanation: Mridula Sarabhai led the movement to shut down foreign cloth and liquor shops in Ahmedabad during the Civil Disobedience Movement, promoting the boycott of British goods.

questions.question: Which dynasty ruled parts of present-day Gujarat between the 10th and 13th centuries and is renowned for constructing the Modhera Sun Temple?
questions.explanation: Between the 10th and 13th centuries, parts of present-day Gujarat and Rajasthan were governed by the Solanki dynasty, known for their architectural contributions like the Modhera Sun Temple and their role in regional politics.

questions.question: In which year was the Indian Institute of Management Ahmedabad (IIM Ahmedabad) established?
questions.explanation: IIM Ahmedabad, established in 1961, is a premier management institute founded through collaboration between the Government of India, Government of Gujarat, and Harvard Business School.

questions.question: Which Indian state is located to the northeast of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Gujarat shares its borders with three Indian states: Rajasthan to the northeast, Madhya Pradesh to the east, and Maharashtra to the southeast.

questions.question: Which port city in Gujarat is historically renowned for its significant role in international maritime trade?
questions.explanation: Gujarat’s economy historically thrived on maritime trade routes, utilizing its extensive coastline and key ports like Surat and Khambhat for international commerce.
